text:
Spider × Yorknew × Assemble (クモ×ヨークシン×全員集合, Kumo × Yōkushin × Zenin Shūgō) is the 51 st episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It first aired on December 23 rd , 2000.

Overview

Neon and her bodyguards arrive in Yorknew City. Neon can foretell the future, and Mafia members hire her to tell fortunes. Phantom Troupe members Feitan, Machi, Franklin, and Nobunaga walkthrough Yorknew City. In the marketplace, Gon runs into Leorio. He helps raise funds by having people pay to arm wrestle Gon, who easily beats everyone, but has trouble defeating Shizuku.

Synopsis

An airship containing Neon and her team approach Yorknew City. Kurapika goes off to make a phone call, and when he comes back to join the other bodyguards, he spends his time thinking about his upcoming meeting with Hisoka and his future battle with the Phantom Troupe.

The airship lands, and Neon rides in a car driven by Ivlenkov with Dalzollene and Linssen. Dalzollene gives Neon her stack of work papers for the month from people who want their fortunes told, and Neon is unhappy with the amount of work she has been assigned. She calls her father to complain, and after some arguing, he agrees to buy her something in exchange, and she is appeased. Neon then uses her Nen ability to fill the papers with fortunes. Dalzollene collects them as she does this and picks up one of the fortunes that get dropped, shocked by what he sees on the paper.

In another car, Basho and Kurapika talk about Neon's ability and her father's connections to the Mafia Community. Neon finishes with her fortune-telling, and Dalzollene agrees to take her shopping.

Nobunaga, Feitan, Franklin, and Machi walk down the road together, talking about the upcoming Spider meeting. Franklin asks if Hisoka will be there, but Machi isn't sure if he'll show. While debating Hisoka's strengths and abilities, Franklin claims Hisoka isn't that tough, and he and Nobunaga get into a brawl. Machi and Feitan speculate about what Chrollo plans to have them steal at the auction, and Feitan is particularly interested in a dangerous game that he knows will be up for auction. Chrollo, Pakunoda, and Kortopi arrive at their hideout to see Hisoka already there.

At the Hotel Beitacle, Dalzollene speaks on the phone with Light, worried over some of the fortunes he saw Neon write that contained the exact same lines as each other, especially since they all hinted at death or illness. Light tells him to send the clients their fortunes and warn them. Dalzollene, concerned with Neon's safety since she cannot predict her own future and since the auction will be a dangerous place, asks if he should still take her there, and Light instructs him to bring her home instead. In addition, he tells Dalzollene to get Neon whatever she wants and to retrieve the items from the auction himself so that Neon will not be upset about not going.

At the Phantom Troupe meeting, Chrollo informs the other troupe members that he plans for them to steal everything available at the auction and that they are permitted to kill everyone else there while doing so.

The next day, Gon and Killua look through a market in town, trying to find another way to make money after they both failed in their endeavors; Gon had only been able to make fifteen thousand , and Killua had made two hundred million jenny gambling only to lose it all, making Gon the winner of their bet. The boys stop at a stall selling cellphones, with Gon intending to buy one to stay in touch with Leorio and Kurapika while they're on the road. After looking for a bit, Leorio shows up at the stall with some helpful advice on cell phones, and the boys are excited to see him. Leorio negotiates with the stall merchant and gets the boys a deal on some cellphones. Leorio asks Killua and Gon if they've learned Nen and informs them that he has already learned it.

Dalzollene gives Neon's bodyguards the rundown on what their next assignment is, which involves buying the items Neon desires from the auction and securing them that night since Neon won't be attending, and informs them of the potential attack that may happen at the auction. He runs through the list of items with them, and when he brings up the Scarlet Eyes, Melody notices Kurapika's heartbeat race. Ivlenkov, Baise, and Tocino get assigned to be bidders at the auction.

In their hotel room, Gon, Killua, and Leorio try to find a way to earn the necessary money to bid for Greed Island at the Southernpiece Auction and look online again. While looking, Leorio gets an idea of how they can make money and takes the boys to the streets. They set up a table on the curb where anyone can pay a fee to arm wrestle Gon, and if the participant wins, they get a diamond ring that the boys purchased earlier. Many men immediately pay the fee and challenge Gon, thinking he'll be easy to beat; none of them succeed.

Shizuku approaches the table and respectfully challenges Gon. Gon genuinely struggles against her, but ultimately wins, although he and Killua are left surprised at how strong she is.

Back at the Hotel Beitacle, Neon freaks out when told she can't attend the auction herself. Dalzollene, who has large bumps on his head due to Neon's outburst, calls her father.
